title: Module 5 handout. Presentation sprint.
subtitle: Brief → storyline → draft → QC (25–30 min)
toolsIntro: AI (structure and content): ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini. Tool tree: Gamma (recommended) · Copilot/Office · Canva. Others as needed.
toolsBullets: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini – structure and content
structure8:
1. Problem / context
2. Why it matters now
3. Solution idea
4. How it works (simply)
5. Example / use case
6. Benefits / results
7. Risks / limits
8. What to do next (CTA)
masterPrompt: Create an 8-slide presentation structure (not full content). Topic: [Enter]. Audience: [Enter]. For each slide provide: title and 2–3 key points. Format: numbered list. Tone: clear, non-tech.
fullPromptPrinciple: META + INPUT + OUTPUT + REASONING + QUALITY + ADVANCED. Storyline: decision + 3 arguments + CTA; titles = assertions.
sequenceSteps: Brief – topic, audience, goal, slide count, tone
briefDefinition: Brief orients AI: topic, audience, goal, slide count, tone/format. Storyline adds: what the audience must decide.
thresholdsExplanation: ≥70% – you understand brief, storyline, structure and quality check; you can proceed to Module 6. Below 70% – review the sprint, QC lab and the indicated slides.
footerText: Prompt anatomy – prompt structure training. © Course material.
